asset.title: L6 Long Horizon
asset.purpose: Silent workflow preview generated from app captures or trace replays. Judge whether it communicates the workflow and gate clearly.

judge.summary: A highly polished, step-by-step visualization of an AI agent resolving multi-cell write conflicts using CAS (Compare-And-Swap) and compaction. The progression is clear, legible, and directly relevant to transactional agent workflows.

featureClarity.evidence: The step-by-step execution clearly demonstrates conflict detection, stale write rejection, and eventual successful commit.

workflowCompleteness.evidence: Shows the entire lifecycle of reading, encountering a conflict, compacting, and successfully writing across multiple cells.

visualDesign.evidence: Clean dark-mode aesthetic with clear row highlighting and a structured progress bar at the bottom.

consistency.evidence: Aligns well with NodeRoom's spreadsheet-based agent coordination and trace-logging paradigm.

evidenceQuality.evidence: Feels like a high-fidelity execution trace replay, though slightly abstracted from a live browser environment.

legibility.evidence: High-contrast text and clear labels make it easy to read even at smaller sizes.

professionalRelevance.evidence: Highly relevant to developers building multi-agent systems requiring transactional integrity over shared state.

productionHonesty.evidence: Explicitly highlights conflicts and retries rather than presenting a flawless, instantaneous execution.

observedEvidence: Conflict state indicators (V1, V2, V3 versions)
Agent action logs at the bottom (reads, writes, compaction)

defects.observed: The interface is highly stylized and abstracted, which may not perfectly match the actual production UI.
defects.fix: Add a small caption or subtitle indicating this is a trace visualization of the underlying agent protocol.

judge.suggestedReadmeCaption: NodeRoom agent executing a long-horizon task: resolving concurrent human-agent edit conflicts on financial cells using CAS and context compaction without locking.
